DocsPipelinesVersion 1
DocsPipelinesVersion 1

Pipeline Version 1

Version 1 is the original pipeline format. It executes steps sequentially on the host machine or an existing container image. All keys documented here are available in version: 1 pipelines.

Looking for parallel jobs, SSH, or Dockerfile support?

Those features require Version 2. See the feature comparison for details.

A complete example showing the major features together:

name: Build project
version: 1
runs_on: ubuntu

variables:
  branch: master

environment:
  BUILD_ENV: production

external:
- name: setup
  pipeline: setup.yaml

artifacts:
- method: get
  from: /output
  to: /local/output
  after: Build step

steps:
- name: Clone repository
  exec:
  - git clone -b bld:var:branch https://example.com/repo

- name: Build step
  working_dir: /repo
  exec:
  - make build

version

version integer required V1

Declares this pipeline as version 1. Must be set to 1 for the V1 runner to execute it. Pipeline versions are independent of the bld release version.

version: 1

name V1 + V2

name string optional V1, V2

Printed at the start of the pipeline’s execution. Supports expressions — variables, environment variables, and keywords can be used in the value.

name: My pipeline

runs_on V1 + V2

runs_on string | map required V1, V2

Defines where pipeline commands execute. Version 1 supports machine and existing container image targets.

Machine

Commands execute directly on the host machine. Bld creates a temporary directory at .bld/tmp/<uuid> for the run, which is cleaned up afterward.

runs_on: machine

Container — Existing Image

Creates a container from an image that must already exist locally. You are responsible for maintaining the image.

runs_on: ubuntu

Note

Pulling images automatically, building from a Dockerfile, or running on a remote host via SSH require Version 2.

dispose V1 + V2

dispose boolean optional V1, V2
Default: false

When set to true, bld will not clean up the platform after a run finishes.

For the machine platform, the temporary directory under .bld/tmp/ is preserved. For the container platform, the container is not stopped or removed. The dispose field has no effect on the SSH platform (version 2 only) since the connection closes when the run ends.

dispose: true

variables V1 + V2

variables map optional V1, V2

A key-value map where each key is a variable name and the value is its default. Variables can be overridden at runtime with the -v flag.

Reference a variable with the bld:var:name prefix:

name: Example pipeline
version: 1
runs_on: machine

variables:
  directory: /home/user/target_directory

steps:
- name: List target directory
  exec:
  - ls bld:var:directory

Override at runtime:

bld run -p example.yaml -v directory=/home/user/another_directory

Multiple variables:

variables:
  first_directory: /home/user/target_directory
  second_directory: /home/user/another_directory
  third_directory: /home/user/yet_another_directory

steps:
- name: List all directories
  exec:
  - ls bld:var:first_directory
  - ls bld:var:second_directory
  - ls bld:var:third_directory

Note

Variable default values support expressions — you can reference other variables, environment variables, or built-in keywords as a default value.

environment V1 + V2

environment map optional V1, V2

A key-value map of environment variables injected into the pipeline run. Supports the bld:env:name expression prefix or the native shell form $name.

name: Example pipeline
version: 1
runs_on: machine

environment:
  first_directory: /home/user/target_directory
  second_directory: /home/user/another_directory

steps:
- name: List directories
  exec:
  - ls bld:env:first_directory
  - ls $second_directory

Override at runtime with -e or --environment:

bld run -p example.yaml -e first_directory=some_path -e second_directory=some_other_path

Note

Environment variable default values support expressions — variables, other environment variables, or keywords can be used.

keywords V1 only

(built-in keywords) expression optional V1

Built-in read-only values available in any pipeline expression. In version 1, keywords use the bld:run: prefix.

KeywordDescription
bld:run:root_dirPath to the .bld directory of the project
bld:run:idUnique UUID for this pipeline run
bld:run:start_timeStart time of the pipeline run
version: 1
runs_on: machine

steps:
- exec:
  - echo bld:run:root_dir
  - echo bld:run:id
  - echo bld:run:start_time

Note

Version 2 adds a fourth keyword (bld_project_dir) and uses a different ${{bld_*}} syntax. See Version 2 keywords.

external V1 + V2

external list optional V1, V2

Declares references to other pipelines (local or on a remote server) that can be invoked from steps using ext:.

Simple inline call — no external section needed:

runs_on: ubuntu
version: 1

steps:
- exec:
  - ext: child.yaml

Named external with variables — define an entry so you can pass variables or environment variables:

runs_on: ubuntu
version: 1

external:
- pipeline: child.yaml
  variables:
    some-variable: an example value

steps:
- exec:
  - ext: child.yaml

Named entries with aliases — useful when the same pipeline is called with different configurations:

runs_on: ubuntu
version: 1

variables:
  some-variable: an example value

external:
- name: Call child.yaml with a variable
  pipeline: child.yaml
  variables:
    some-variable: bld:var:some-variable

- name: Call child.yaml on a server
  pipeline: child.yaml
  server: demo_server
  variables:
    some-variable: an example value

steps:
- exec:
  - ext: Call child.yaml with a variable
  - ext: Call child.yaml on a server

You can also pass environment variables to child pipelines:

external:
- name: Execute child pipeline
  pipeline: child.yaml
  variables:
  - variable1: hello
  environment:
  - env1: hello world

Note

The external section supports expressions in name, server, pipeline, variables, and environment fields.

artifacts V1 + V2

artifacts list optional V1, V2

Transfers files to or from the execution platform during a run. Supports get (retrieve from container) and push (send to container).

Retrieve a file from a container after a step completes:

name: Example pipeline
runs_on: ubuntu
version: 1

artifacts:
- method: get
  from: /sample_file
  to: /home/user/some_directory
  after: Echo step

steps:
- name: Echo step
  exec:
  - echo 'hello world' > sample_file
- name: Another step
  exec:
  - echo 'hello again'

The after field specifies the step name after which the transfer occurs. Omit after to run the transfer before any steps execute. The from and to fields support expressions.

steps V1 only

steps list required V1

Defines the sequential execution units of the pipeline. Steps run one after another in the order they are defined.

Note

Version 2 replaces steps with jobs for parallel execution. See Version 2 jobs.

Simple exec

The simplest form — a list of shell commands under exec:

name: Example pipeline
runs_on: machine
version: 1

steps:
- exec:
  - ls ./some_directory
  - ls ./some_other_directory

- exec:
  - echo 'hello world' > sample_file

- exec:
  - curl ifconfig.me

Named steps

Give a step a name and an exec list for clarity and artifact targeting:

name: Example pipeline
runs_on: machine
version: 1

steps:
- name: List directories
  exec:
  - ls ./some_directory
  - ls ./some_other_directory

- name: Create file
  exec:
  - echo 'hello world' > sample_file

Working directory

Use working_dir to set the working directory for a step’s commands:

name: Example pipeline
runs_on: ubuntu
version: 1

steps:
- name: First step
  working_dir: /some/working/directory
  exec:
  - ls ./subdirectory
  - rm ./subdirectory/file

- name: Second step
  working_dir: /another/working/directory
  exec:
  - ls ./some-other-dir/

Calling external pipelines in steps

Use ext: inside an exec list to invoke another pipeline:

name: Example pipeline
runs_on: machine
version: 1

steps:
- name: Call another pipeline
  exec:
  - ext: example-1.yaml
  - ls ./some_directory

Note

Named steps can be referenced from the artifacts section to transfer files after that step completes.

validation

Pipelines are validated before every run. Validation also runs when a pipeline is pushed to a bld server, or manually via bld check.

external → pipeline

Checks if the pipeline file path exists.

external → server

Checks if the server name is found in .bld/config.yaml.

steps → exec → ext

Checks if the external key is found either as a local pipeline file or a declared entry in the external section.

artifacts → after

Checks if the step name in the after field is found in the pipeline’s steps.

Previous Pipelines Next Version 2

Simple and blazingly fast CI/CD.
Run pipelines anywhere.
